extra.reason: Added automated tx_* schema contract tests that compare TxSchemas against the canonical migration, including exact table coverage and column order. These guards now protect reliability refactors from accidental schema drift without changing the database contract.

Added a regression harness for current parsing and loading assumptions: representative sample response parsing is now asserted via E2E characterization, and schema contract guards were added so refactors preserve current successful load behavior.

Implemented explicit pipeline statuses (completed/partial/failed), propagated partial results through webhook reporting, added error breakdown and sample diagnostics, and covered partial-failure behavior with unit tests. Pipeline no longer reports full success when operational errors occurred.

Removed unsafe background use of ResponseWriter by making /api/files synchronous, replaced the one-shot worker lifecycle with a long-lived draining queue worker, rejected new enqueues during shutdown, and wired server signal handling into predictable graceful shutdown. Added tests for synchronous download failure, shutdown rejection, and queue drain behavior.

Hardened parser critical-field handling by rejecting malformed core tx fields instead of silently coercing them, increased scanner buffer size for long Frontol lines, and added quarantine semantics for repeated parse-failure files via durable local lifecycle state. Added tests for critical-field validation, long-line parsing, and poison-pill file quarantine.

Made startup validation fail fast for malformed numeric environment variables, made KASSA_STRUCTURE explicit and required, applied strict env parsing to main config, DB config, and FTP config, and expanded tests to cover invalid integer envs and malformed kassa structure.

Made loader behavior deterministic by sorting transaction tables before load/export statistics, extracted explicit retry policy, removed large unused legacy loader paths, and added unit tests for deterministic order, retry backoff, retryable retries, and non-retryable stop behavior without changing tx_* schema compatibility.

Improved operator-visible diagnostics by adding kassa/folder-level processing summaries to pipeline results and webhook reports, exposing shutdown state in queue and health status, and extending tests so the new observability artifacts are preserved across ETL and HTTP status surfaces.

Completed the file lifecycle reliability work by adding durable manifest-based reconciliation for corrected reuploads. The system now persists per-file transaction manifests, detects duplicate content vs corrected content by remote-path latest state, finalizes duplicate reuploads without reloading, and deletes stale rows in the same transaction before loading corrected file content. Added unit coverage for replay recovery, poison-pill quarantine, corrected reupload reconciliation, and deterministic stale-row deletion order.

Expanded the reliability safety net by introducing focused regression and race targets in the Makefile, wiring GitHub Actions to run the stronger CI path, and fixing or documenting the lint blockers so make ci now passes locally with lint, focused regressions, and race detection on critical packages.

Untangled the webhook-server control flow by splitting queue/runtime lifecycle and download/export logic out of the oversized main file into dedicated package-local files. This keeps the behavior and tests intact while making the server boundaries and operational flow easier to follow and evolve.

Synchronized configuration, testing, API, architecture, and coding-rule documentation with the reliability refactor. Docs now reflect strict config validation, load-only queue semantics, synchronous download behavior, new CI/test commands, updated validation package naming, and the new ETL result diagnostics.

Removed the legacy parser transaction mapper layer while preserving the shared numeric parsing helper used by the current tx_* parser, and deleted the old table-specific database load layer in favor of the schema-driven LoadTxTable path. Simplified db interfaces and mocks to the current production flow, updated tests, and verified the cleanup with go test ./... and make ci.

Reduced the /api/files export memory footprint by replacing the full in-memory TXT buffer with a bounded buffered streaming writer that sends Frontol-formatted output directly to the HTTP response. The API contract and file format are preserved, skipped-row diagnostics are retained in bounded form, and the change is covered by focused streaming tests plus full go test ./... and make ci verification.

Further decomposed webhook-server by moving report delivery flow, health/status handlers, documentation handlers, and server bootstrap/runtime wiring out of the oversized main.go into dedicated package-local files. API behavior remained unchanged and the refactor was verified with go test ./... and make ci.

Reviewed docs/IMPROVEMENTS.md, marked already completed reliability items as historical, documented that active backlog now lives in bd, and converted the still-relevant near-term items into concrete tasks: metrics, rate limiting/audit logging, COPY evaluation, and timeout unification.

Implemented Loki-friendly operational logging instead of a separate metrics stack. Added LOG_FORMAT support with JSON as the default structured format, emitted stable ETL/webhook summary events such as etl_run_summary, etl_kassa_summary, and download_export_summary, and documented how to use the structured fields with Loki/Grafana.

Reworked the security follow-up into Loki-friendly webhook audit logging instead of rate limiting. Added structured API audit events for request receipt/completion/rejection, enriched auth failure logs with request metadata, and kept the output detailed but bounded for Loki/Grafana usage without leaking secrets.

Unified timeout management across DB, FTP, webhook HTTP server, webhook report delivery, CLI entrypoints, and per-file ETL load stages. Added explicit timeout configuration fields with stable defaults, validated them on startup, documented the timeout map, logged active runtime timeout settings on startup, and verified the change with go test -run=^$ ./..., golangci-lint run, and go test ./....

Updated Dockerfile, release workflow, Makefile, and developer command references to build command package directories instead of individual main.go files. Verified by rebuilding the same Docker/release/local binaries successfully after the webhook-server package split.
Reapplied on top of current main, verified with go test ./..., golangci-lint run, and go build ./...
Reapplied on top of current main, verified with go test ./..., golangci-lint run, and go build ./...

Blocked load queue from advancing on webhook timeout before pipeline completion, added regression coverage for timeout overlap semantics, verified with go test ./..., golangci-lint run, and go build ./...
Scoped file lifecycle and reconcile state by requested date so sequential multi-date loads no longer delete earlier days, verified with go test ./..., golangci-lint run, and go build ./...

Added DB-backed durable file load state written in the same transaction as tx_* rows, switched duplicate/reconcile decisions to durable state, added migration/docs/tests, verified with go test ./..., golangci-lint run, and go build ./...
Implemented and verified with go test ./..., golangci-lint run, and go build ./...
Implemented and verified with go test ./..., golangci-lint run, and go build ./...
